evidence_excerpt: By age 32, echocardiography revealed a mildly dilated aortic root and ascending aorta with mild aortic regurgitation; she also continues to have thrombocytopenia with easy bruising and prolonged bleeding.

quality_of_life.evidence_excerpt: She has episodic headache with photophobia and phonophobia and continues to have easy bruising and prolonged bleeding time.

timeline_events.evidence_excerpt: An echocardiography at age 32 years revealed a mildly dilated aortic root and ascending aorta with mild aortic regurgitation.
She continues to have easy bruising and prolonged bleeding time (persistent thrombocytopenia).
She has episodic headache with photophobia and phonophobia.

notes_on_uncertainty: Index time was approximated to the PNH diagnosis at age 24 after MRI obtained for a first seizure. The report provides limited longitudinal detail on seizure control and exact onset timing of headaches; thrombocytopenia was noted before index time but is documented as persistent afterward. Cardiac findings were detected at age 32; prior cardiac status for dilation/regurgitation was not documented, so this was treated as a long-term complication.
